Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets analyze the traces from the event log Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC Notice that all cases start with RA VF AE

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then there is a loop involving RMI and VF

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Wait in Case 1 AE RMI VF In Case 2 AE no RMI AD In Case 3 AE RMI RMI VF AD

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The loop is usually defined as A B where A is executed then either exit or execute B and then A again

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: If A is tau and B is RMI that does not work well

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If A tau and B RMI then it produces RMI RMI RMI but we need VF after RMI

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: If A tau and B sequence RMI VF

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Then it would produce RMI VF RMI VF but Case 3 is RMI RMI VF

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: So B cannot be sequence RMI VF

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Notice that in Case 3 RMI happens twice then VF

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: What if we have X tau sequence loop tau RMI then VF

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 chooses the sequence branch loop tau RMI produces RMI then VF which matches Case 2 chooses tau which matches no RMI no VF Case 3 chooses the sequence branch loop tau RMI produces RMI RMI then VF which matches

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets double check loop tau RMI A tau B RMI executes tau then exit or RMI then tau and this exactly matches the number of RMIs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So sequence loop tau RMI then VF produces VF or RMI VF or RMI RMI VF and when wrapped in XOR with tau it models both the optionality and the repetitions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets write out the full tree Sequence RA VF AE XOR tau sequence loop tau RMI then VF AD SC AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC correct Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC correct Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC correct
